projectDescription: An interpretable framework for extracting spectral feature embeddings from 1D time-series and time-frequency representations, designed for geophysical signal characterization and clustering.
keyFeatures: Pointwise spectral feature extraction from 1D time series and 2D spectrograms via GISQAPipeline
Pairwise metrics using noise bank comparison, GMM labeling, and GLSZM spatial features
STFT and CWT spectrogram generation with configurable frequency ranges and normalization
50+ information-theoretic, entropy, complexity, and statistical features
Hilbert curve encoding for spatial flattening of 2D time-frequency representations
Applications for DAS arrays, seismic event catalog clustering, and synthetic waveform analysis
Physics-based acoustic wave propagation simulation through heterogeneous media
SHAP-based feature importance and interpretability tools
